questions.question: 中文导读：What is the difference between a probability mass function (PMF) and a probability density function (PDF)?
options: 中文选项：PMFs are used for continuous variables, PDFs for discrete variables
中文选项：PMFs give exact probabilities for discrete outcomes, while PDFs give densities for continuous variables that must be integrated over an interval to get probability
中文选项：There is no difference; they are the same concept with different names
中文选项：PMFs always sum to 0.5, PDFs integrate to 1
questions.explanation: 中文解析：For discrete variables, the PMF gives P(X=k) directly. For continuous variables, the PDF f(x) is a density -- P(a<=X<=b) requires integrating f(x) from a to b. The density at a single point is not a probability.

questions.question: 中文导读：What does the Central Limit Theorem state?
options: 中文选项：All data follows a normal distribution
中文选项：The mean of many independent random samples converges to a normal distribution regardless of the source distribution
中文选项：Large datasets always have low variance
中文选项：The probability of rare events decreases as sample size increases
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The CLT says that the average of many independent random variables approaches a Gaussian, no matter what the original distribution looks like. This explains why the normal distribution appears everywhere.

questions.question: 中文导读：Why does softmax subtract the maximum logit before exponentiating (the 'softmax trick')?
options: 中文选项：To make all probabilities equal
中文选项：To prevent numerical overflow from exponentiating large numbers while producing mathematically identical results
中文选项：To normalize the logits to have mean zero
中文选项：To speed up the computation by reducing the number of exponentiations
questions.explanation: 中文解析：exp(100) overflows to infinity. Subtracting max(logits) shifts all values so the largest is 0. exp(0)=1 is safe. The subtraction cancels out in the normalization, giving identical probabilities.

questions.question: 中文导读：Cross-entropy loss for classification simplifies to -log(q(true_class)). What does this mean intuitively?
options: 中文选项：Multiply the predicted probabilities by the true labels
中文选项：Penalize the model based on how low its predicted probability is for the correct class -- lower prediction means higher loss
中文选项：Average the log probabilities across all classes
中文选项：Compute the entropy of the true distribution
questions.explanation: 中文解析：If the model predicts 0.9 for the correct class, loss = -log(0.9) = 0.105 (low). If it predicts 0.01, loss = -log(0.01) = 4.6 (high). The loss punishes low confidence in the correct answer.

questions.question: 中文导读：Why do language models work with log probabilities instead of raw probabilities?
options: 中文选项：Log probabilities are easier to interpret visually
中文选项：Multiplying many small probabilities causes numerical underflow to zero; log probabilities convert products to sums, avoiding this
中文选项：Log probabilities are required by the transformer architecture
中文选项：Raw probabilities cannot represent values less than 0.01
questions.explanation: 中文解析：P(sentence) = P(word1) * P(word2) * ... quickly underflows to 0.0 with float64. Log P(sentence) = log P(word1) + log P(word2) + ... stays in a finite range. Products become sums.
